task_completion.description: The GMAT script is saved to the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of a file named 'lunar_transfer.script' saved in the '~/Documents/missions/' directory (e.g., in a file manager, terminal, or GMAT save dialog).

task_completion.description: The GMAT script includes the required simulation components
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence within the GMAT GUI or script editor showing the configuration of a multi-body force model, TLI and LOI impulsive burns, and a targeting sequence (DifferentialCorrector or Optimizer).

task_completion.description: The GMAT simulation is successfully run
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the GMAT simulation executing or having completed, such as a 3D graphics window displaying an Earth-to-Moon trajectory plot or a message indicating a successful run.

task_completion.description: The mission results file is created in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of a file named 'lunar_transfer_results.txt' existing in the '~/GMAT_output/' directory.

task_completion.description: The final mission results are recorded with the required metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the contents of 'lunar_transfer_results.txt' showing the required keys: TLI_DeltaV_mps, LOI_DeltaV_mps, Total_DeltaV_mps, TransferTime_days, and FinalLunarAlt_km, populated with numerical values.

integrity.description: The agent actually used GMAT to generate the trajectory and results
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the GMAT application (either GUI or running it via CLI) rather than just fabricating the results in a text editor.

integrity.description: The recorded results match the GMAT simulation output
integrity.visual_evidence: The values written to 'lunar_transfer_results.txt' should plausibly correspond to the outputs or reports generated by the GMAT run, without signs of being arbitrarily guessed.

integrity.description: The GMAT script contains genuine simulation logic
integrity.visual_evidence: The script file 'lunar_transfer.script' should contain actual GMAT syntax and logic for an Earth-Moon transfer, not just empty or dummy content.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The mission specification defines an initial LEO SMA of 6556.14 km and inclination of 28.5 degrees. The expected results in '/home/ga/GMAT_output/lunar_transfer_results.txt' should fall within these ranges: TLI Delta-V between 2800 and 3500 m/s, LOI Delta-V between 500 and 1200 m/s, Total Delta-V between 3500 and 4500 m/s, Transfer Time between 2 and 7 days, and Final Lunar Altitude between 50 and 200 km.
